description_long: This object is a vintage television with a boxy, rectangular form and a woodgrain exterior finish, reminiscent of models from the late 20th century. The front features a large, slightly convex glass screen occupying most of the surface, bordered by a thick wooden bezel. To the right of the screen, there is a vertical panel with a speaker grille above and a control section below, which includes several buttons and possibly a rotary dial. The sides and back are solid wood-textured panels, with the rear section protruding outward to accommodate internal components. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a robust, heavy appearance. This television would likely be found in older urban interiors, garages, or as discarded street furniture.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, large glass screen, and side control panel featuring buttons and a speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large glass screen, wood bezel, speaker grille, and control panel with buttons on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the woodgrain texture and the protruding rear section, with no visible controls or screen.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the deep, boxy back panel with continuous woodgrain finish and no visible ports or features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the woodgrain surface and the stepped profile of the rear housing.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark brown woodgrain bezel with sharp edges and a matte finish.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of buttons and possibly a dial on the lower right front panel.
